title: Moods, Emotions and Feelings
justification: This talk provides the most comprehensive and structured exploration of desire, craving, and letting go, using the memorable 'Little Lily and the ice cream' narrative as a unifying thread. It systematically traces the psychology of desire from initial wanting through attachment, frustration, anger, depression, and finally acceptance—covering the full emotional cycle with vivid examples. The talk connects personal psychology to Buddhist doctrine (the Second Noble Truth, sankhara, samsara vs. nibbana) while maintaining accessibility, and concludes with practical meditation guidance on experiencing states at the bodily feeling level rather than through thought.
summary: This Foundation Course talk uses the extended metaphor of a child wanting ice cream to illuminate the entire psychology of desire, attachment, and suffering. It traces how sensual craving creates emotional cycles of expectation, frustration, anger, and depression, while offering the meditative path of experiencing these states as bodily feelings rather than indulging or repressing them.

relevance.note: Primarily about sensual desires creating emotional patterns and suffering
